title: Chapter 791

One of the guards, the youngest, had leaned in just a little too far. It wasn't conscious intention. It was stupid curiosity, the kind that arises on its own. His eyes lingered on the bars for a fraction of a second longer than they should have… and he saw enough.

His eyes widened.

He paled.

And immediately he turned his face so forcefully that he almost dislocated his neck.

"By all the gods…" he murmured, swallowing hard.

The other guard, noticing the sudden panic, frowned.

"What? What is it?"

The first didn't answer immediately. He just rubbed his face with his hand, as if trying to erase the image from his mind. Then he took a step back, leaning against the cold stone wall.

"No… don't look," he said in a low voice. "Seriously. Don't look."

That, of course, was the worst possible advice.

The second guard hesitated for half a second… and then did the exact opposite. A quick glance. An irreversible mistake.

He saw intertwined bodies, heard muffled laughter, felt the atmosphere too heavy to be mistaken for anything else—and immediately turned away as well, almost tripping over his own spear.

The two now had their backs to the cell, staring at the wall as if it were the most interesting thing ever built by civilization.

The silence between them lasted a few awkward seconds.

"…"

"…"

Then the older one cleared his throat.

"We…" he began, choosing his words with extreme care, "…didn't see anything."

The younger man nodded too quickly.

"Nothing. Absolutely nothing."

They exchanged a sideways glance, the kind of glance that carries envy, disbelief, and bitter resignation.

"Even so…" murmured the young man, almost against his will, "…what a lucky bastard."

The other let out a tired sigh.

"Lucky?" he repeated. "Imprisoned for multiple murders, dangerous as a natural disaster… and yet…"

He shook his head slowly.

"Some men are born with everything."

"Others are born to be prison guards," replied the young man bitterly.
